entities.name: Ticket
suggestedFollowupTemplates: Compare {entity} this week vs last week
entities.description: Support ticket — the central business entity.

entities.name: User
entities.description: THE PRIMARY 'users' table. When the user asks about 'users', 'staff', 'agents', 'admins' without further qualification, THIS is the correct table — NOT Customers (those are external utility subscribers). Internal staff (ASP.NET Identity) handle Tickets and run admin workflows. Disambiguation rule: 'users' alone always means AspNetUsers; 'customers'/'subscribers'/'complainants' means Customers.

entities.name: TicketComment
entities.description: User-authored comments on tickets. The DB has no separate Messages or Conversations table — comments on tickets ARE the message/reply concept here.

entities.name: TicketStatus
entities.description: Lookup table — Open, In Progress, Resolved, Closed, etc.

entities.name: TicketPriority
entities.description: Lookup table — Low, Medium, High, Critical.

entities.name: TicketCategory
entities.description: Lookup table — ticket subject area.

entities.name: TicketAttachment
entities.description: Files uploaded to a ticket — has FileName, FilePath, ContentType (e.g. image/png, application/pdf), FileSize (bytes, bigint), UploadedByUserId, UploadedAt, optional CommentId. Use FileSize for 'larger than X MB' filters (1 MB = 1048576 bytes); use ContentType for grouping by media kind.

entities.name: TicketAiAnalysis
entities.description: AI-produced analyses of tickets — has Summary, SuggestedClassification, SuggestedPriority, ConfidenceLevel (Low/Medium/High/Critical, nvarchar), ModelName (e.g. 'qwen2.5:3b'), ProcessingDurationMs (bigint), AnalysisStatus, RunNumber. Group by ConfidenceLevel / ModelName / AnalysisStatus for distribution questions; use ProcessingDurationMs for latency questions.

entities.name: Role
entities.description: ASP.NET Identity role — columns are Id (string PK), Name, NormalizedName, ConcurrencyStamp. Use Name when grouping or filtering by role (e.g. 'Admin', 'Agent', 'Customer'). Use AspNetUserRoles as the bridge to count users per role.

entities.name: UserRole
entities.description: Many-to-many bridge between AspNetUsers and AspNetRoles. Columns: UserId (FK → AspNetUsers.Id), RoleId (FK → AspNetRoles.Id). Use this to count users per role: SELECT r.Name, COUNT(*) FROM AspNetUserRoles ur JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId GROUP BY r.Name.

entities.name: Customer
entities.description: External customer (utility subscriber) — distinct from ApplicationUser (internal staff). FullNameEn and FullNameAr are the bilingual name. NationalId is a fake 11-digit identifier. Customers consume services (Electricity, Internet, Water, Gas) which produce Bills, and may file Tickets. Status is the canonical lifecycle filter (values: 'Active', 'Suspended', 'Churned'). ChurnedAt timestamp is set ONLY when Status='Churned' — to find churned customers use Status='Churned' NOT ChurnedAt=value. Likewise filter 'no email' with Email IS NULL, not Email=value. RegionId points at the customer's district (Region.RegionType = District); join Region → ParentRegion to get the governorate.

entities.name: Bill
entities.description: Utility bill -- single polymorphic table covering all four ServiceType values (Electricity, Internet, Water, Gas). All monetary fields in Syrian Pounds (SYP). Status is the canonical payment filter (values: 'Issued' current, 'Paid', 'Overdue' past-due, 'Disputed', 'Cancelled'). PaidAt is just a completion timestamp set when Status='Paid' — to find paid bills use Status='Paid' NOT PaidAt=value. Linked to Customer (CustomerId) and the Department that issued it (DepartmentId). UsageQuantity + UsageUnit describe consumption in kWh/GB/m³/cylinders depending on ServiceType.

entities.name: Department
entities.description: Regional service operator -- one Department per (Region, ServiceType) pair. Total 56 (14 governorates × 4 service types: Electricity/Internet/Water/Gas). Each Bill is issued by a Department; each Ticket is handled by a Department. The dimension along which super-admin Copilot questions pivot for cross-region/cross-service comparisons. RegionId points at the governorate (not the district).

entities.name: Country
entities.description: Country lookup. Currently a single row: Syria / سوريا / SYR. Region.CountryId references this.

entities.name: Region
entities.description: Hierarchical geography: RegionType=Governorate rows (14 of them: Damascus, Aleppo, Homs, ...) sit at the top with ParentRegionId=null. RegionType=District rows (~62) point at their governorate via ParentRegionId. Customers reference a district (RegionId); Departments reference a governorate (RegionId). To get a customer's governorate, join Region → ParentRegion.

entities.name: ServiceType
entities.description: Lookup of utility service types. Seeded values: Electricity, Internet, Water, Gas, GovernmentProcess. Code is the stable machine identifier; NameEn/NameAr are display labels. Admins add new types via /ReferenceData without a code change. Referenced by Bill, Department, Outage, MeterReading, Tariff, and (optionally) TicketCategory.

entities.name: ComplaintType
entities.description: Lookup of complaint classifications. Seeded codes: ServiceDown, ServiceDegraded, BillingDispute, MeterIssue, Disconnection, NewConnection, Other. Tickets carry an optional ComplaintTypeId for the dominant complaint nature.

entities.name: ResolutionType
entities.description: Lookup of how a ticket was closed. Codes: Resolved, NoFault, BillAdjusted, Escalated, Cancelled, OutageCleared. Group by this to analyze closure patterns (e.g. tickets cleared as 'NoFault' vs genuine 'Resolved').

entities.name: Outage
entities.description: Explicit service outage events (electricity blackouts, internet fiber cuts, water pipe bursts, etc.). Tickets can attribute to a specific OutageId. Use Severity (Minor/Moderate/Major/Critical), Cause (FiberCut/PipeBurst/Equipment/Weather/...), and DATEDIFF(hour, StartedAt, EndedAt) for MTTR analysis. Group by RegionId/ServiceTypeId for hot-spot analysis.

entities.name: MeterReading
entities.description: Periodic consumption readings per (Customer, ServiceType). Value is cumulative; Consumption is the computed delta from the previous reading. ReaderType: Actual (physical/smart-meter), Estimated (computed), Customer (self-reported), Audit (verification). Use for anomaly detection ('customers whose consumption spiked 3x') and to validate Bills against actual usage.

entities.name: CsatResponse
entities.description: Customer satisfaction survey response attached to a resolved Ticket. Score 1-5 (1=very dissatisfied, 5=very satisfied). Sentiment: Negative/Neutral/Positive. One CSAT per Ticket maximum. Used to measure Copilot answer quality and ticket-resolution effectiveness. Average Score by Department, by ComplaintType, by Region for breakdown analysis.

entities.name: Tariff
entities.description: Historical pricing per (ServiceType, Region). RegionId=null means a country-wide tariff. EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo windows tell you which tariff applied during a bill's period. Use to EXPLAIN bill changes — when Bill.TotalAmount jumped, find the Tariff with EffectiveFrom near that date and surface its ChangeReasonEn.

metrics.name: open_ticket_count
metrics.description: Number of tickets in the Open status.

metrics.name: closed_ticket_count
metrics.description: Number of tickets in the Closed status.

metrics.name: resolved_ticket_count
metrics.description: Number of tickets in the Resolved status.

metrics.name: ticket_count
metrics.description: Total number of tickets.

metrics.name: avg_resolution_hours
synonyms: how long does it take to fix an issue on average
how long does it take to resolve an issue on average
metrics.description: Average hours from ticket creation to resolution. Skips unresolved tickets.

metrics.name: avg_ticket_age_days
metrics.description: Average age of tickets in days from CreatedAt until now.

metrics.name: tickets_per_category
metrics.description: Used with groupBy=[TicketCategories.Name] to count tickets per category. Reach for this when the user asks 'Categories with at least N tickets' / 'tickets per category' — root MUST be Tickets, not TicketCategories.

metrics.name: tickets_per_priority
metrics.description: Used with groupBy=[TicketPriorities.Name]. Reach for this when the user asks 'Priorities with N tickets' / 'tickets per priority' — root MUST be Tickets, not TicketPriorities.

metrics.name: tickets_per_status
metrics.description: Used with groupBy=[TicketStatuses.Name]. Reach for this when the user asks 'Statuses with more than N tickets' / 'tickets per status' — root MUST be Tickets, not TicketStatuses.

metrics.name: attachment_count
metrics.description: Total count of TicketAttachments. Root must be TicketAttachments.

metrics.name: large_attachment_count
metrics.description: Count of attachments larger than 1 MB (FileSize > 1,048,576 bytes). Root must be TicketAttachments.

metrics.name: total_attachment_size_mb
metrics.description: Sum of all attachment FileSize in megabytes. Root must be TicketAttachments.

metrics.name: avg_attachment_size_kb
metrics.description: Average attachment FileSize in kilobytes. Root must be TicketAttachments.

metrics.name: max_attachment_size_mb
metrics.description: Size of the single largest attachment in MB. Root must be TicketAttachments.

metrics.name: attachments_per_content_type
metrics.description: Used with groupBy=[TicketAttachments.ContentType]. Root must be TicketAttachments — count attachments per MIME content type.

metrics.name: ai_analysis_count
metrics.description: Total count of TicketAiAnalyses rows. Root must be TicketAiAnalyses.

metrics.name: successful_ai_analysis_count
metrics.description: Count of AI analyses where AnalysisStatus = 'Success'. Root must be TicketAiAnalyses.

metrics.name: failed_ai_analysis_count
metrics.description: Count of AI analyses where AnalysisStatus = 'Failed'. Root must be TicketAiAnalyses.

metrics.name: avg_ai_processing_ms
synonyms: average processing time of ai analyses
metrics.description: Mean ProcessingDurationMs across all AI analyses (milliseconds). Root must be TicketAiAnalyses.

metrics.name: max_ai_processing_ms
synonyms: maximum processing duration across ai analyses
metrics.description: Largest ProcessingDurationMs (slowest run) in milliseconds. Root must be TicketAiAnalyses.

metrics.name: analyses_per_confidence_level
metrics.description: Used with groupBy=[TicketAiAnalyses.ConfidenceLevel]. Root must be TicketAiAnalyses — count analyses bucketed by Low / Medium / High / Critical.

metrics.name: analyses_per_model
metrics.description: Used with groupBy=[TicketAiAnalyses.ModelName]. Root must be TicketAiAnalyses — count runs per LLM model.

metrics.name: high_confidence_analysis_count
metrics.description: Count of AI analyses with ConfidenceLevel in {High, Critical}. Root must be TicketAiAnalyses.

metrics.name: role_count
metrics.description: Total number of rows in AspNetRoles. Root must be AspNetRoles.

metrics.name: user_count
metrics.description: Total number of rows in AspNetUsers (internal staff — agents, supervisors, admins). NOT for external customers / utility subscribers (use the Customer entity + Customers table for those). Root must be AspNetUsers.

metrics.name: users_per_role
metrics.description: Used with groupBy=[AspNetRoles.Name]. Root MUST be AspNetUserRoles, joined to AspNetRoles. Counts how many user assignments exist for each role name — answers 'how many users for each role'.

metrics.name: customer_count
metrics.description: Total number of rows in Customers (external utility subscribers). Root must be Customers. NOT for internal staff (use user_count for that).

dimensions.name: ticket_age_days
dimensions.description: Days between ticket creation and now.

dimensions.name: ticket_age_hours
dimensions.description: Hours between ticket creation and now.

dimensions.name: created_month
dimensions.description: Calendar month bucket for the CreatedAt column.

dimensions.name: content_type
dimensions.description: MIME content type of the attachment (e.g. image/png, application/pdf, application/zip). Use as groupBy when bucketing attachments by media kind.

dimensions.name: attachment_size_mb
dimensions.description: Per-row attachment FileSize converted to megabytes (FLOAT). Use in select/orderBy when the user wants 'size in MB' shown on each row.

dimensions.name: attachment_size_kb
dimensions.description: Per-row attachment FileSize converted to kilobytes (FLOAT).

dimensions.name: confidence_level
dimensions.description: AI analysis confidence (Low / Medium / High / Critical, nvarchar). Use as groupBy for distribution questions.

dimensions.name: analysis_model
dimensions.description: Name of the LLM that produced the analysis (e.g. 'qwen2.5:3b'). Use as groupBy when comparing models.

dimensions.name: analysis_status
dimensions.description: Outcome of the AI analysis run (Success / Failed / Pending / Processing).

dimensions.name: role_name
dimensions.description: Role name from AspNetRoles (e.g. 'Admin', 'Agent', 'Customer'). Use as groupBy when bucketing users by their role assignment.

conceptPatterns.meaning: tickets with no assigned user (AssignedToUserId IS NULL)
open tickets older than 7 days — not yet resolved
tickets past their due date and still unresolved
bills with overdue status (issued and not yet paid past the due date)
bills with status issued or overdue (any state that is not paid)
tickets in Open or In Progress status
outages that have started but not yet ended (EndedAt IS NULL)
tickets that have not been resolved (ResolvedAt IS NULL)
count of tickets per user/agent — measures individual workload
entity with the highest ticket count
tickets that were resolved but moved back to open
tickets with zero comments — no one has responded yet. Use LEFT JOIN TicketComments ON Tickets.Id = TicketComments.TicketId WHERE TicketComments.Id IS NULL
tickets with attachments larger than 1 MB
